title: Wall Street Crash of 1929
description: Triggered the Great Depression. Dow fell ~89%. Black Thursday marked the start; Black Tuesday (Oct 29) was the worst day. Recovery took decades.

title: Black Monday
description: Dow dropped 22% in one day — the largest single-day percentage decline in history. Caused by program trading and panic selling.

title: Dot-com Bubble Burst
description: Tech stock speculation peaked. Nasdaq fell ~78% from March 2000 to October 2002. Many internet companies collapsed.

title: Lehman & Global Financial Crisis
description: Lehman Brothers collapse triggered global financial panic. Housing and banking crisis. S&P 500 fell ~57%. Led to massive government bailouts.

title: September 11 Attacks
description: Markets closed for days. Increased volatility and uncertainty. NYSE closed until Sept 17.

title: Brexit Referendum
description: UK voted to leave the EU. Currency and European market turbulence. Sterling fell sharply.

title: Russian Invasion of Ukraine
description: Energy price shocks, commodity volatility. Global inflation pressure. Markets initially fell, then fluctuated.

title: COVID-19 Stock Market Crash
description: Fastest bear market in history. Sharp V-shaped recovery after stimulus. S&P 500 fell ~34% in about a month.

title: Volcker Shock
description: Fed raised interest rates dramatically to combat inflation. Rates peaked above 19% in 1981. Caused recession but achieved long-term stability.

title: Quantitative Easing
description: Fed announced QE1 — massive liquidity injections post-2008. Boosted asset prices globally. Multiple rounds through 2014.

title: Silicon Valley Bank Collapse
description: Second-largest bank failure in US history. Regional banking instability. Short-term market volatility. FDIC took over.
